NKJV.1: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming upon you!
NKJV.2: Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are moth-eaten.
NKJV.3: Your gold and silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have heaped up treasure in the last days.
NKJV.4: Indeed the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, cry out; and the cries of the reapers have reached the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth.
NKJV.5: You have lived on the earth in pleasure and luxury; you have fattened your hearts as in a day of slaughter.
NKJV.6: You have condemned, you have murdered the just; he does not resist you.
NKJV.7: Therefore be patient, brethren, until the coming of the Lord. See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, waiting patiently for it until it receives the early and latter rain.
NKJV.8: You also be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.
NKJV.9: Do not grumble against one another, brethren, lest you be condemned. Behold, the Judge is standing at the door!
NKJV.10: My brethren, take the prophets, who spoke in the name of the Lord, as an example of suffering and patience.
NKJV.11: Indeed we count them blessed who endure. You have heard of the perseverance of Job and seen the end intended by the Lord—that the Lord is very compassionate and merciful.
NKJV.12: But above all, my brethren, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or with any other oath. But let your “Yes” be “Yes,” and your “No,” “No,” lest you fall into judgment.
NKJV.13: Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing psalms.
NKJV.14: Is anyone among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord.
NKJV.15: And the prayer of faith will save the sick, and the Lord will raise him up. And if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
NKJV.16: Confess your trespasses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The effective, fervent prayer of a righteous man avails much.
NKJV.17: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it would not rain; and it did not rain on the land for three years and six months.
NKJV.18: And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
NKJV.19: Brethren, if anyone among you wanders from the truth, and someone turns him back,
NKJV.20: let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and cover a multitude of sins.

ESV.1: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for the miseries that are coming upon you.
ESV.2: Your riches have rotted and your garments are moth-eaten.
ESV.3: Your gold and silver have corroded, and their corrosion will be evidence against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up treasure in the last days.
ESV.4: Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, are crying out against you, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.
ESV.5: You have lived on the earth in luxury and in self-indulgence. You have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
ESV.6: You have condemned and murdered the righteous person. He does not resist you.
ESV.7: Be patient, therefore, brothers,  until the coming of the Lord. See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient about it, until it receives the early and the late rains.
ESV.8: You also, be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.
ESV.9: Do not grumble against one another, brothers, so that you may not be judged; behold, the Judge is standing at the door.
ESV.10: As an example of suffering and patience, brothers, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
ESV.11: Behold, we consider those blessed who remained steadfast. You have heard of the steadfastness of Job, and you have seen the purpose of the Lord, how the Lord is compassionate and merciful.
ESV.12: But above all, my brothers, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or by any other oath, but let your “yes” be yes and your “no” be no, so that you may not fall under condemnation.
ESV.13: Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing praise.
ESV.14: Is anyone among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord.
ESV.15: And the prayer of faith will save the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. And if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
ESV.16: Therefore, confess your sins to one another and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person has great power as it is working.
ESV.17: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed fervently that it might not rain, and for three years and six months it did not rain on the earth.
ESV.18: Then he prayed again, and heaven gave rain, and the earth bore its fruit.
ESV.19: My brothers, if anyone among you wanders from the truth and someone brings him back,
ESV.20: let him know that whoever brings back a sinner from his wandering will save his soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.

NASB.1: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries which are coming upon you.
NASB.2: Your riches have rotted and your garments have become moth-eaten.
NASB.3: Your gold and your silver have rusted; and their rust will be a witness against you and will consume your flesh like fire. It is in the last days that you have stored up your treasure!
NASB.4: Behold, the pay of the laborers who mowed your fields, [and] which has been withheld by you, cries out [against you]; and the outcry of those who did the harvesting has reached the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth.
NASB.5: You have lived luxuriously on the earth and led a life of wanton pleasure; you have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
NASB.6: You have condemned and put to death the righteous [man]; he does not resist you.
NASB.7: Therefore be patient, brethren, until the coming of the Lord. The farmer waits for the precious produce of the soil, being patient about it, until it gets the early and late rains.
NASB.8: You too be patient; strengthen your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is near.
NASB.9: Do not complain, brethren, against one another, so that you yourselves may not be judged; behold, the Judge is standing right at the door.
NASB.10: As an example, brethren, of suffering and patience, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
NASB.11: We count those blessed who endured. You have heard of the endurance of Job and have seen the outcome of the Lord's dealings, that the Lord is full of compassion and [is] merciful.
NASB.12: But above all, my brethren, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or with any other oath; but your yes is to be yes, and your no, no, so that you may not fall under judgment.
NASB.13: Is anyone among you suffering? [Then] he must pray. Is anyone cheerful? He is to sing praises.
NASB.14: Is anyone among you sick? [Then] he must call for the elders of the church and they are to pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord;
NASB.15: and the prayer offered in faith will restore the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up, and if he has committed sins, they will be forgiven him.
NASB.16: Therefore, confess your sins to one another, and pray for one another so that you may be healed. The effective prayer of a righteous man can accomplish much.
NASB.17: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it would not rain, and it did not rain on the earth for three years and six months.
NASB.18: Then he prayed again, and the sky poured rain and the earth produced its fruit.
NASB.19: My brethren, if any among you strays from the truth and one turns him back,
NASB.20: let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save his soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.

NLT.1: Look here, you rich people: Weep and groan with anguish because of all the terrible troubles ahead of you.
NLT.2: Your wealth is rotting away, and your fine clothes are moth-eaten rags.
NLT.3: Your gold and silver are corroded. The very wealth you were counting on will eat away your flesh like fire. This corroded treasure you have hoarded will testify against you on the day of judgment.
NLT.4: For listen! Hear the cries of the field workers whom you have cheated of their pay. The cries of those who harvest your fields have reached the ears of the LORD of Heaven’s Armies.
NLT.5: You have spent your years on earth in luxury, satisfying your every desire. You have fattened yourselves for the day of slaughter.
NLT.6: You have condemned and killed innocent people, who do not resist you.
NLT.7: Dear brothers and sisters, be patient as you wait for the Lord’s return. Consider the farmers who patiently wait for the rains in the fall and in the spring. They eagerly look for the valuable harvest to ripen.
NLT.8: You, too, must be patient. Take courage, for the coming of the Lord is near.
NLT.9: Don’t grumble about each other, brothers and sisters, or you will be judged. For look — the Judge is standing at the door!
NLT.10: For examples of patience in suffering, dear brothers and sisters, look at the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
NLT.11: We give great honor to those who endure under suffering. For instance, you know about Job, a man of great endurance. You can see how the Lord was kind to him at the end, for the Lord is full of tenderness and mercy.
NLT.12: But most of all, my brothers and sisters, never take an oath, by heaven or earth or anything else. Just say a simple yes or no, so that you will not sin and be condemned.
NLT.13: Are any of you suffering hardships? You should pray. Are any of you happy? You should sing praises.
NLT.14: Are any of you sick? You should call for the elders of the church to come and pray over you, anointing you with oil in the name of the Lord.
NLT.15: Such a prayer offered in faith will heal the sick, and the Lord will make you well. And if you have committed any sins, you will be forgiven.
NLT.16: Confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The earnest prayer of a righteous person has great power and produces wonderful results.
NLT.17: Elijah was as human as we are, and yet when he prayed earnestly that no rain would fall, none fell for three and a half years!
NLT.18: Then, when he prayed again, the sky sent down rain and the earth began to yield its crops.
NLT.19: My dear brothers and sisters, if someone among you wanders away from the truth and is brought back,
NLT.20: you can be sure that whoever brings the sinner back from wandering will save that person from death and bring about the forgiveness of many sins.

WEB.1: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming on you.
WEB.2: Your riches are corrupted and your garments are moth-eaten.
WEB.3: Your gold and your silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be for a testimony against you, and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up your treasure in the last days.
WEB.4: Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you have kept back by fraud, cry out, and the cries of those who reaped have entered into the ears of the Lord of Armies.
WEB.5: You have lived delicately on the earth, and taken your pleasure. You have nourished your hearts as in a day of slaughter.
WEB.6: You have condemned, you have murdered the righteous one. He doesn’t resist you.
WEB.7: Be patient therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient over it, until it receives the early and late rain.
WEB.8: You also be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.
WEB.9: Don’t grumble, brothers, against one another, so that you won’t be judged. Behold, the judge stands at the door.
WEB.10: Take, brothers, for an example of suffering and of perseverance, the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
WEB.11: Behold, we call them blessed who endured. You have heard of the perseverance of Job, and have seen the Lord in the outcome, and how the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
WEB.12: But above all things, my brothers, don’t swear — not by heaven, or by the earth, or by any other oath; but let your “yes” be “yes”, and your “no”, “no”; so that you don’t fall into hypocrisy.
WEB.13: Is any among you suffering? Let him pray. Is any cheerful? Let him sing praises.
WEB.14: Is any among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord,
WEB.15: and the prayer of faith will heal him who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
WEB.16: Confess your offenses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The insistent prayer of a righteous person is powerfully effective.
WEB.17: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain, and it didn’t rain on the earth for three years and six months.
WEB.18: He prayed again, and the sky gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
WEB.19: Brothers, if any among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back,
WEB.20: let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.

CSB17.1: Come now, you rich <sup>ⓛ</sup> people, weep and wail over the miseries that are coming on you.
CSB17.2: Your wealth has rotted and your clothes are moth-eaten. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: Your gold and silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have stored up treasure in the last days. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.4: Look! The pay that you withheld from the workers <sup>ⓐ</sup> who mowed your fields cries out, and the outcry of the harvesters has reached the ears of the Lord of Hosts. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.5: You have lived luxuriously on the earth and have indulged yourselves. You have fattened your hearts <sup>ⓒ</sup> in a day of slaughter. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.6: You have condemned, you have murdered the righteous, who does not resist you. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.7: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be patient until the Lord’s coming. See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth and is patient with it until it receives the early and the late rains. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.8: You also must be patient. Strengthen your hearts, because the Lord’s coming is near. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.9: Brothers and sisters, do not complain about one another, so that you will not be judged. Look, the judge stands at the door! <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.10: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the Lord’s name as an example of suffering and patience.
CSB17.11: See, we count as blessed those who have endured. <sup>[2]</sup> You have heard of Job’s endurance <sup>ⓘ</sup> and have seen the outcome that the Lord brought about — the Lord is compassionate and merciful. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.12: Above all, my brothers and sisters, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or with any other oath. <sup>ⓚ</sup> But let your “yes” mean “yes,” and your “no” mean “no,” so that you won’t fall under judgment. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.13: Is anyone among you suffering? He should pray. Is anyone cheerful? He should sing praises. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.14: Is anyone among you sick? He should call for the elders of the church, and they are to pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.15: The prayer of faith will save the sick person, and the Lord will raise him up; if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.16: Therefore, confess your sins to one another and pray for one another, so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is very powerful in its effect. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.17: Elijah was a human being as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it would not rain, and for three years and six months it did not rain on the land. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.18: Then he prayed again, and the sky gave rain and the land produced its fruit. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.19: My brothers and sisters, if any among you strays from the truth, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and someone turns him back, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.20: let that person know that whoever turns a sinner from the error of his way will save his soul from death and cover a multitude of sins. <sup>ⓕ</sup>

AMP.1: Come [quickly] now, you rich [who lack true faith and hoard and misuse your resources], weep and howl over the miseries [the woes, the judgments] that are coming upon you.
AMP.2: Your wealth has rotted and is ruined and your [fine] clothes have become moth-eaten.
AMP.3: Your gold and silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you and will consume your flesh like fire. You have stored up your treasure in the last days [when it will do you no good].
AMP.4: Look! The wages that you have [fraudulently] withheld from the laborers who have mowed your fields are crying out [against you for vengeance]; and the cries of the harvesters have come to the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth.
AMP.5: On the earth you have lived luxuriously and abandoned yourselves to soft living and led a life of wanton pleasure [self-indulgence, self-gratification]; you have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
AMP.6: You have condemned and have put to death the righteous man; he offers you no resistance.
AMP.7: So wait patiently, brothers and sisters, until the coming of the Lord. The farmer waits [expectantly] for the precious harvest from the land, being patient about it, until it receives the early and late rains.
AMP.8: You too, be patient; strengthen your hearts [keep them energized and firmly committed to God], because the coming of the Lord is near.
AMP.9: Do not complain against one another, believers, so that you will not be judged [for it]. Look! The Judge is standing right at the door.
AMP.10: As an example, brothers and sisters, of suffering and patience, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord [as His messengers and representatives].
AMP.11: You know we call those blessed [happy, spiritually prosperous, favored by God] who were steadfast and endured [difficult circumstances]. You have heard of the patient endurance of Job and you have seen the Lord's outcome [how He richly blessed Job]. The Lord is full of compassion and is merciful.
AMP.12: But above all, my fellow believers, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or with any other oath; but let your yes be [a truthful] yes, and your no be [a truthful] no, so that you may not fall under judgment.
AMP.13: Is anyone among you suffering? He must pray. Is anyone joyful? He is to sing praises [to God].
AMP.14: Is anyone among you sick? He must call for the elders (spiritual leaders) of the church and they are to pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord;
AMP.15: and the prayer of faith will restore the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up; and if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
AMP.16: Therefore, confess your sins to one another [your false steps, your offenses], and pray for one another, that you may be healed and restored. The heartfelt and persistent prayer of a righteous man (believer) can accomplish much [when put into action and made effective by God—it is dynamic and can have tremendous power].
AMP.17: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours [with the same physical, mental, and spiritual limitations and shortcomings], and he prayed intensely for it not to rain, and it did not rain on the earth for three years and six months.
AMP.18: Then he prayed again, and the sky gave rain and the land produced its crops [as usual].
AMP.19: My brothers and sisters, if anyone among you strays from the truth and falls into error and [another] one turns him back [to God],
AMP.20: let the [latter] one know that the one who has turned a sinner from the error of his way will save that one's soul from death and cover a multitude of sins [that is, obtain the pardon of the many sins committed by the one who has been restored].

MSG.1: And a final word to you arrogant rich: Take some lessons in lament. You'll need buckets for the tears when the crash comes upon you.
MSG.2: Your money is corrupt and your fine clothes stink.
MSG.3: Your greedy luxuries are a cancer in your gut, destroying your life from within. You thought you were piling up wealth. What you've piled up is judgment.
MSG.4: All the workers you've exploited and cheated cry out for judgment. The groans of the workers you used and abused are a roar in the ears of the Master Avenger.
MSG.5: You've looted the earth and lived it up. But all you'll have to show for it is a fatter than usual corpse.
MSG.6: In fact, what you've done is condemn and murder perfectly good persons, who stand there and take it.
MSG.7: Meanwhile, friends, wait patiently for the Master's Arrival. You see farmers do this all the time, waiting for their valuable crops to mature, patiently letting the rain do its slow but sure work.
MSG.8: Be patient like that. Stay steady and strong. The Master could arrive at any time.
MSG.9: Friends, don't complain about each other. A far greater complaint could be lodged against you, you know. The Judge is standing just around the corner.
MSG.10: Take the old prophets as your mentors. They put up with anything, went through everything, and never once quit, all the time honoring God.
MSG.11: What a gift life is to those who stay the course! You've heard, of course, of Job's staying power, and you know how God brought it all together for him at the end. That's because God cares, cares right down to the last detail.
MSG.12: And since you know that he cares, let your language show it. Don't add words like "I swear to God" to your own words. Don't show your impatience by concocting oaths to hurry up God. Just say yes or no. Just say what is true. That way, your language can't be used against you.
MSG.13: Are you hurting? Pray. Do you feel great? Sing.
MSG.14: Are you sick? Call the church leaders together to pray and anoint you with oil in the name of the Master.
MSG.15: Believing-prayer will heal you, and Jesus will put you on your feet. And if you've sinned, you'll be forgiven—healed inside and out.
MSG.16: Make this your common practice: Confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you can live together whole and healed. The prayer of a person living right with God is something powerful to be reckoned with.
MSG.17: Elijah, for instance, human just like us, prayed hard that it wouldn't rain, and it didn't—not a drop for three and a half years.
MSG.18: Then he prayed that it would rain, and it did. The showers came and everything started growing again.
MSG.19: My dear friends, if you know people who have wandered off from God's truth, don't write them off. Go after them. Get them back
MSG.20: and you will have rescued precious lives from destruction and prevented an epidemic of wandering away from God.

NIV.1:
Warning to Rich Oppressors
Now listen, you rich people, weep and wail because of the misery that is coming upon you.
NIV.2: Your wealth has rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
NIV.3: Your gold and silver are corroded. Their corrosion will testify against you and eat your flesh like fire. You have hoarded wealth in the last days.
NIV.4: Look! The wages you failed to pay the workmen who mowed your fields are crying out against you. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord Almighty.
NIV.5: You have lived on earth in luxury and self-indulgence. You have fattened yourselves in the day of slaughter.
NIV.6: You have condemned and murdered innocent men, who were not opposing you.
NIV.7:
Patience in Suffering
Be patient, then, brothers, until the Lord’s coming. See how the farmer waits for the land to yield its valuable crop and how patient he is for the autumn and spring rains.
NIV.8: You too, be patient and stand firm, because the Lord’s coming is near.
NIV.9: Don’t grumble against each other, brothers, or you will be judged. The Judge is standing at the door!
NIV.10: Brothers, as an example of patience in the face of suffering, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
NIV.11: As you know, we consider blessed those who have persevered. You have heard of Job’s perseverance and have seen what the Lord finally brought about. The Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
NIV.12: Above all, my brothers, do not swear—not by heaven or by earth or by anything else. Let your “Yes” be yes, and your “No,” no, or you will be condemned.
NIV.13:
The Prayer of Faith
Is any one of you in trouble? He should pray. Is anyone happy? Let him sing songs of praise.
NIV.14: Is any one of you sick? He should call the elders of the church to pray over him and anoint him with oil in the name of the Lord.
NIV.15: And the prayer offered in faith will make the sick person well; the Lord will raise him up. If he has sinned, he will be forgiven.
NIV.16: Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous man is powerful and effective.
NIV.17: Elijah was a man just like us. He prayed earnestly that it would not rain, and it did not rain on the land for three and a half years.
NIV.18: Again he prayed, and the heavens gave rain, and the earth produced its crops.
NIV.19: My brothers, if one of you should wander from the truth and someone should bring him back,
NIV.20: remember this: Whoever turns a sinner from the error of his way will save him from death and cover over a multitude of sins.

NRSVCE.1: Come now, you rich people, weep and wail for the miseries that are coming to you.
NRSVCE.2: Your riches have rotted, and your clothes are moth-eaten.
NRSVCE.3: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you, and it will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up treasure for the last days.
NRSVCE.4: Listen! The wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, cry out, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.
NRSVCE.5: You have lived on the earth in luxury and in pleasure; you have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
NRSVCE.6: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who does not resist you.
NRSVCE.7: Be patient, therefore, beloved, until the coming of the Lord. The farmer waits for the precious crop from the earth, being patient with it until it receives the early and the late rains.
NRSVCE.8: You also must be patient. Strengthen your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is near.
NRSVCE.9: Beloved, do not grumble against one another, so that you may not be judged. See, the Judge is standing at the doors!
NRSVCE.10: As an example of suffering and patience, beloved, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
NRSVCE.11: Indeed we call blessed those who showed endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job, and you have seen the purpose of the Lord, how the Lord is compassionate and merciful.
NRSVCE.12: Above all, my beloved, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or by any other oath, but let your “Yes” be yes and your “No” be no, so that you may not fall under condemnation.
NRSVCE.13: Are any among you suffering? They should pray. Are any cheerful? They should sing songs of praise.
NRSVCE.14: Are any among you sick? They should call for the elders of the church and have them pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
NRSVCE.15: The prayer of faith will save the sick, and the Lord will raise them up; and anyone who has committed sins will be forgiven.
NRSVCE.16: Therefore confess your sins to one another, and pray for one another, so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous is powerful and effective.
NRSVCE.17: Elijah was a human being like us, and he prayed fervently that it might not rain, and for three years and six months it did not rain on the earth.
NRSVCE.18: Then he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain and the earth yielded its harvest.
NRSVCE.19: My brothers and sisters, if anyone among you wanders from the truth and is brought back by another,
NRSVCE.20: you should know that whoever brings back a sinner from wandering will save the sinner’s soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.

NET.1: Come now, you rich! Weep and cry aloud over the miseries that are coming on you.
NET.2: Your riches have rotted and your clothing has become moth-eaten.
NET.3: Your gold and silver have rusted and their rust will be a witness against you. It will consume your flesh like fire. It is in the last days that you have hoarded treasure!
NET.4: Look, the pay you have held back from the workers who mowed your fields cries out against you, and the cries of the reapers have reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.
NET.5: You have lived indulgently and luxuriously on the earth. You have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
NET.6: You have condemned and murdered the righteous person, although he does not resist you.
NET.7: So be patient, brothers and sisters, until the Lord’s return. Think of how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the ground and is patient for it until it receives the early and late rains.
NET.8: You also be patient and strengthen your hearts, for the Lord’s return is near.
NET.9: Do not grumble against one another, brothers and sisters, so that you may not be judged. See, the judge stands before the gates!
NET.10: As an example of suffering and patience, brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the Lord’s name.
NET.11: Think of how we regard as blessed those who have endured. You have heard of Job’s endurance and you have seen the Lord’s purpose, that the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
NET.12: And above all, my brothers and sisters, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or by any other oath. But let your “Yes” be yes and your “No” be no, so that you may not fall into judgment.
NET.13: Is anyone among you suffering? He should pray. Is anyone in good spirits? He should sing praises.
NET.14: Is anyone among you ill? He should summon the elders of the church, and they should pray for him and anoint him with oil in the name of the Lord.
NET.15: And the prayer of faith will save the one who is sick and the Lord will raise him up — and if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
NET.16: So confess your sins to one another and pray for one another so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person has great effectiveness.
NET.17: Elijah was a human being like us, and he prayed earnestly that it would not rain and there was no rain on the land for three years and six months!
NET.18: Then he prayed again, and the sky gave rain and the land sprouted with a harvest.
NET.19: My brothers and sisters, if anyone among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back,
NET.20: he should know that the one who turns a sinner back from his wandering path will save that person’s soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.
